trip.title: Japan Trip 2026
changelog: v2.3: May 19 rebuilt — tightened schedule, depart Kyoto evening to Osaka. Nishiki Market trimmed to 30 min graze, Gion antiques 20-30 min browse, Maruyama Park 20-30 min. Yasaka Shrine / Hanamikoji / Kamo River walk removed (done May 16). Farewell Kyoto dinner moved to 16:30. Train to Osaka on May 19 evening. May 20 now full Osaka day — no morning Kyoto checkout travel.

v2.2: May 16 rebuilt — early checkout from Fujiya, afternoon Shinkansen to Kyoto, Higashiyama evening (Kiyomizu-dera, Ninenzaka, Yasaka, Hanamikoji). Pola Museum and Tenzan Tohji-kyo removed (onsen_3 dropped). May 17 rebuilt — Amanohashidate + Ine full day organised tour. Gran Class Shinkansen (train_3) removed from May 17. Dormy Inn hotel_3 check-in moved to May 16.

v2.1: May 15 rebuilt — Shinjuku → Kawaguchiko (bus), Chureito Pagoda, Kachi Kachi Ropeway + Love Bell, Lake Kawaguchiko north shore, Owakudani, Hakone Shrine, Fujiya Hotel. Romance Car removed. Pirate Ship and Lake Ashi rowboat removed.
v2.1: May 16 rebuilt — full rest day at Fujiya. Dawn onsen, hotel gardens, Gora Park, Pola Museum, afternoon rest, Tenzan Tohji-kyo public onsen at night.

outbound.connection_note: YVR has US Customs Preclearance - clear customs in Vancouver, arrive Tokyo as domestic. Connection is 1h 32m - move quickly.

baggage.notes: 1 checked bag + 1 cabin bag + 1 backpack per person included. Second checked bag $100 per trip.

hotels.name: JR Kyushu Hotel Blossom Shinjuku
hotels.notes: 3 min walk from Shinjuku Station South Exit. Luggage storage available before check-in and after check-out.
hotels.checkin_note: Check-in from 2 PM. 3-min walk from JR Shinjuku Station south exit.

hotels.name: Fujiya Hotel (est. 1878)
hotels.onsen_description: Historic mineral pools - indoor and outdoor rotenburo
hotels.notes: Japan's oldest Western hotel. Yukata and geta provided. 3 min walk from Miyanoshita Station.
hotels.checkin_note: Standard checkout 11 AM — ask reception about 12 PM late checkout.

hotels.name: Dormy Inn Kyoto Gion
hotels.notes: 5 min walk to Hanamikoji Street. Free late-night ramen 9:30-11 PM nightly. Check-in May 16 (one night earlier than original).
hotels.checkin_note: Check-in from 3 PM. Gion area — Hanamikoji St 5 min walk. Free late-night ramen 9:30–11 PM.

hotels.name: Residence Hotel Takayama Station
hotels.checkin_time_note: Front desk open 6:30 AM–midnight only. Early departures: drop keys in key box.
hotels.notes: 30 seconds from Takayama Station. Full apartment with kitchen. Front desk NOT 24 hours (6:30 AM–midnight).
hotels.checkin_note: Check-in from 3 PM. 30 seconds from Takayama Station. Bags arrive via TA-Q-BIN (sent May 18). Checkout May 22.

hotels.name: Yuen Shinjuku
hotels.onsen_description: Rooftop onsen - indoor and outdoor with Tokyo skyline views
hotels.checkin_time_note: Early check-in NOT possible per hotel FAQ. Luggage storage available before check-in.
hotels.notes: 8 min walk from Shinjuku Station. Yukata provided. Onsen open 15:00-02:00 and 06:00-10:00.
hotels.checkin_note: Check-in from 3 PM May 22. Rooftop onsen open late. Suzuka race day May 23 — early start.

onsens.type: Historic mineral pools - indoor + outdoor rotenburo
onsens.notes: First onsen of the trip! Historic mineral baths since 1878.
Dawn rotenburo - almost empty at this hour.

onsens.type: Indoor + outdoor with Tokyo skyline views
onsens.notes: Best onsen views of the trip. Shinjuku skyline at night.

trains.notes: Activate JR Pass at Narita JR counter BEFORE boarding.

trains.seat_tip: RIGHT side for Mt Fuji view ~13:45 between Odawara and Shin-Fuji — may be your last Fuji view of the trip.
trains.book_at: JR office at Odawara Station on arrival
trains.notes: Departs Odawara ~13:10. No Gran Class on this service — ordinary reserved is comfortable. Reserve at Odawara JR office.
Heading east (Tokyo direction). Reserved seat. Quick transfer at Nagoya to Wide View Hida.

trains.seat_tip: LEFT side A/B seats - Hida River gorge appears ~90 min in.
trains.notes: JR Pass. LEFT side for Kiso Valley river gorge views. Arrives Takayama ~12:38.

trains.seat_tip: LEFT side A/B seats - gorge views on return too.
trains.notes: Last mountain train. Hida river gorge views all the way to Nagoya.

trains.seat_tip: Any reserved seat. Take Hikari — NOT Nozomi (JR Pass not valid on Nozomi).
trains.notes: Depart Tokyo/Shinagawa ~06:50. Arrive Nagoya ~09:00. Transfer to Kintetsu for Shiroko.

trains.jr_pass_warning: NOT covered by JR Pass - buy separately at Kintetsu office east side Nagoya Station. ~Y1,720 each way. Buy BOTH directions at same time.
trains.notes: NOT JR Pass. Buy tickets at Kintetsu Nagoya Station. Buy BOTH outbound + return at same time. ~¥1,720 each way.

trains.seat_tip: Any reserved seat - evening train, Fuji view unlikely after dark.
trains.notes: Arrives Tokyo ~13:33. JR Chuo Line to Shinjuku.
Evening return from Suzuka. Depart ~18:30, arrive Tokyo ~20:15.
Depart 14:20 sharp. Return Suica card at Narita JR office for balance + Y500 deposit refund.

itinerary.label: Pre-Departure Night

schedule.activity: Final pack - both checked bags (1 per person)
schedule.notes: Max 23kg / 50lb each. Lay everything out first then pack.

schedule.activity: Final pack - both cabin bags (1 per person)
schedule.notes: Max 10kg. Must fit overhead: 56x36x23 cm.
Under-seat item. Pack tomorrow's snacks + entertainment.
Checked max 23kg, cabin max 10kg. Overweight fees are expensive.

schedule.activity: JR Pass vouchers x2 in carry-on - NEVER checked bag
schedule.notes: Lose these and the trip gets very expensive very fast.
Double check validity - must be valid 6+ months past May 2026.

schedule.activity: Download all booking confirmations offline on phone
schedule.notes: teamLab, Sumo, Romance Car, Tea Ceremony, Suzuka, Air Canada.

schedule.activity: Charge all devices + power banks fully overnight
schedule.notes: Phones, camera, earbuds, power banks - all plugged in.

schedule.activity: Download Japan offline maps in Google Maps (on home WiFi)
schedule.notes: Do this on WiFi - large download.

schedule.activity: Download Japanese language pack in Google Translate (on home WiFi)
schedule.notes: Camera translate is essential for menus and signs.
Front door, back door, garage, all windows.

schedule.activity: Turn off all lights - check every room
Turn off and unplug kitchen appliances
schedule.notes: Oven, toaster, coffee maker - unplug them all.

schedule.activity: Turn off and unplug non-essential electronics
schedule.notes: TVs, lamps, desktop chargers.
May in Texas - set around 78F, no need to run AC hard.

schedule.activity: Empty fridge of anything that will go bad
Schedule Uber for 06:30 AM pickup tomorrow
schedule.notes: Do this in the Uber app tonight - scheduled rides are more reliable.

schedule.activity: Set TWO alarms - 05:45 AM and 06:00 AM
schedule.notes: Early flight - no snoozing.

tips: JR Pass vouchers must be in carry-on - losing them is catastrophic
Schedule Uber tonight rather than requesting in the morning
Leave nothing perishable in the fridge - 2 weeks is a long time

itinerary.label: Departure Day

schedule.notes: Last morning at home for 2 weeks!
Don't forget phone chargers, razors, last-minute toiletries.
Lights off? Doors locked? Oven off? Fridge closed?

schedule.activity: Uber pickup - Cedar Park (Veterans Park area)
schedule.notes: 1 hour to Austin-Bergstrom Airport (AUS).
Head to Air Canada check-in counter.

schedule.activity: Check in + bag drop - Air Canada counter
schedule.notes: 2 checked bags (1 per person, included). Counter closes ~07:50.
AUS is usually fast. Allow 30 min to be safe.

schedule.activity: At gate - grab coffee and last US breakfast

schedule.notes: Boeing 737 MAX 8. 4h 38m. Air Canada Bistro meal available for purchase.
1h 32m connection - move quickly off the plane.

schedule.activity: Clear US Customs Preclearance at YVR
schedule.notes: YVR processes US customs in Canada - you arrive in Tokyo as a domestic passenger. Follow signs.

schedule.activity: Walk to Air Canada international gate
schedule.notes: Terminal M - follow AC Japan signs. YVR is well signed.
Much bigger plane - Dreamliner. Better air quality and humidity than older aircraft.
Boeing 787-9. Wi-Fi available. Two full meals included. 9h 55m.

schedule.activity: Cross International Date Line - Monday becomes Tuesday
schedule.notes: You skip overnight - May 11 ends, May 12 begins while in the air.

tips: YVR connection (1h 32m): deplane fast, follow Air Canada Japan signs, clear customs, go straight to gate
Dreamliner 787-9 tip: window shades are electronic - dim but don't fully block for better sleep
Try to sleep on the Vancouver-Tokyo leg - arrive refreshed

itinerary.label: Arrive Tokyo

schedule.notes: Welcome to Japan! You cleared customs in Vancouver so you walk out as a domestic arrival.
Faster than normal since customs was done at YVR. Allow 30-45 min.

schedule.activity: Activate JR Pass x2 at Narita JR ticket counter
schedule.notes: Ground floor Terminal 1. Show vouchers + passports. This is your most important task on arrival.

schedule.activity: Pick up 2x Suica cards at JR counter
schedule.notes: Load Y5,000 each (~$35). Covers all metro, buses, convenience stores.

schedule.activity: Board N'EX Narita Express to Shinjuku
schedule.notes: Platform B1. 90 min. Covered by JR Pass.
5 min walk to hotel.

schedule.activity: Check in - JR Kyushu Hotel Blossom Shinjuku
schedule.notes: Night 1 of 4.
2 min walk. Smoky yakitori alley. Very atmospheric first Tokyo meal.
8 min walk. 200 tiny bars. Each fits 6-8 people. Just walk in and sit.
Jet lag is real. Tomorrow starts at 10 AM.

tips: Activate JR Pass FIRST before doing anything else at Narita
Golden Gai: cover charge ~$5-8, just pick any bar and sit down
You skipped a night flying so jet lag hits hard tonight - sleep early

itinerary.label: Kimono + Asakusa + teamLab

schedule.notes: Metro: Shinjuku > Ginza > Asakusa. Book at wargokyoto.com in advance.
5 min walk from Wargo. Free entry.
Toei Line > Higashi-Ginza. Fresh tuna, uni, tamagoyaki.
Hibiya Line > Harajuku. Forested approach path.

schedule.activity: Takeshita Street - kawaii fashion and crepes
schedule.notes: 3 min walk from shrine.
12 min walk.

schedule.activity: Starbucks 2nd floor - aerial view of the crossing
schedule.notes: Above Shibuya Crossing.
5 min walk. Solo booth ramen experience.

schedule.activity: teamLab Planets Toyosu - immersive digital art
schedule.notes: Yamanote > Shimbashi > Yurikamome. Timed entry - book at teamlab.art!
Yurikamome > Yamanote.

tips: Wear slip-on shoes today - easy on/off for kimono fitting AND teamLab water rooms
teamLab sells out weeks ahead - must pre-book at teamlab.art

itinerary.label: Shibuya Sky + Sumo Natsu Basho

schedule.notes: JR Yamanote > Shibuya (7 min). Book at shibuya-scramble-square.com.

schedule.activity: 360 degree views + open air rooftop + Mt Fuji view
schedule.notes: Morning gives best Fuji visibility before haze builds.
JR Yamanote > Akihabara > JR Sobu. 35 min.

schedule.activity: Chanko nabe pre-sumo lunch at Tomoegata
schedule.notes: 2 min from Kokugikan. Wrestler's hot pot stew.
Pre-booked at sumo.or.jp.

schedule.activity: Enter Ryogoku Kokugikan - grab arena bento + beer
🏆 Sumo Natsu Basho - Makuuchi top division bouts
schedule.notes: May tournament runs May 11-25. Yokozuna bout ~17:45.
5 min walk from arena.

schedule.activity: JR Sobu Line back to Shinjuku
schedule.notes: 30 min.
2 min from hotel.

tips: Book Shibuya Sky 10:30 AM slot - best Mt Fuji visibility before haze
Arriving at sumo at 2 PM gives full lower + top division experience

itinerary.label: Fuji Day — Chureito + Kachi Kachi + Lake + Owakudani + Hakone Shrine + Fujiya Hotel
itinerary.luggage_note: Send large bags via TA-Q-BIN to Fujiya Hotel at 06:30. Travel daypack only all day.
itinerary.early_start_reason: Mt Fuji clearest before 11 AM — early bus essential for Chureito views

schedule.activity: 🧳 TA-Q-BIN bags forwarded to Fujiya Hotel
schedule.notes: Hotel front desk. Pack daypack night before. Bags arrive Fujiya afternoon (~¥3,500 total).
Convenience store or hotel. Fast — onigiri, coffee, snack for bus.

schedule.activity: Walk to Busta Shinjuku (Shinjuku Expressway Bus Terminal)
schedule.notes: 10 min walk. 4th floor above Shinjuku Station south exit. Pre-booked bus departs here.
Fujikyuko Bus. ~1h 45min. ~¥2,200/person. Pre-book at highway-buses.jp. NOT JR Pass. Direct, no transfers.
Buy Kawaguchiko Retro Bus day pass at station kiosk (~¥1,500/person).
One stop, 10 min, ¥310 each. NOT JR Pass. Buy at machine at Kawaguchiko Station. Or take World Heritage Loop Bus from outside station (15 min, covered by day pass).
10 min walk from station to shrine, then 398 steps up. Take it steady — 20-25 mins to summit. Stand LEFT of pagoda for the classic Fuji shot. No entry fee. Open 24 hours. Bring water.

schedule.activity: Descend and return to Kawaguchiko Station
schedule.notes: Fujikyu Railway or World Heritage Loop Bus back. 10–15 min.

5 min walk from station. ~¥1,000 return (not in day pass). 3 min gondola. Summit: panoramic lake + Fuji view from above, Love Bell — ring it together (couples tradition), summit shrine. Allow 35–40 min.

5 min walk from ropeway base. Houtou noodles (Yamanashi specialty — thick flat noodles in miso-pumpkin broth, gentle on stomach), tempura teishoku, or Yoshida udon. Picture menus. ¥1,200–1,800/person.

schedule.activity: 📸 Oishi Park north shore — Lake Kawaguchiko stroll
schedule.notes: Red Kawaguchiko bus (day pass) 2 stops north. Classic wide-angle Fuji-reflected-in-lake shot. Mid-May: purple and pink lupin wildflowers line the lakefront. 30–40 min.
Fujikyuko Highway Bus to Gotemba (~50 min, ~¥1,500), then Odakyu Highway Bus Gotemba → Hakone-Yumoto (~40 min, ~¥1,000). Total ~1h 45min. Pre-book Kawaguchiko → Gotemba leg at highway-buses.jp.

schedule.activity: Arrive Hakone-Yumoto — buy 2-day Hakone Free Pass
schedule.notes: ¥6,000/person at station kiosk. Covers all Hakone transport for today + May 16: Tozan Railway, cable car, ropeway, all Hakone buses.
40 min. Covered by Hakone Free Pass. Train reverses direction 3 times climbing the mountain — famous engineering. Transfer at Gora to cable car.
Hakone Tozan Cable Car: Gora → Sounzan (10 min, pass). Then Hakone Ropeway: Sounzan → Owakudani (15 min, pass). LAST ROPEWAY ~17:00 — check board at Gora. If closed, fold into May 16 morning instead.

schedule.activity: 🌋 Owakudani Volcanic Valley + Kuro Tamago black eggs
schedule.notes: Japan's most dramatic geothermal landscape. Sulfur vents blast white steam. Buy kuro tamago (black eggs, boiled in volcanic spring, jet-black shells) — legend says each egg adds 7 years to your life. Mt Fuji visible from observation deck on clear days. Allow 30 min. Check last ropeway down time.

schedule.activity: 🚡 Ropeway back → Cable Car → Tozan Railway → Miyanoshita area
schedule.notes: Owakudani → Sounzan by ropeway, Sounzan → Gora cable car (10 min), Gora → Miyanoshita by Tozan Railway (15 min). All Hakone Free Pass.

schedule.activity: 🛕 Hakone Shrine + Peace Torii Gate on Lake Ashi
schedule.notes: Bus from Miyanoshita or Hakone-Yumoto to Hakone-machi (~20 min, pass). Walk 5 min to shrine. Vermilion torii gate standing directly in Lake Ashi — one of Japan's most iconic views. Best at dusk — light is extraordinary, crowds thinned. Cedar-lined approach (350-year-old trees). Allow 40–45 min.
Bus back toward Miyanoshita (pass) or taxi from shrine directly (¥2,000, 15 min). After today — take the taxi.
TA-Q-BIN bags have arrived. Room ready. Receive yukata + geta sandals. Japan's oldest Western hotel — Charlie Chaplin, John Lennon, Helen Keller stayed here. Explore the Meiji-era corridors briefly.

schedule.activity: ♨️ ONSEN 1 — Fujiya Hotel historic mineral pools
schedule.notes: First onsen of the trip! Silky sulphurous mineral water from Hakone hot springs. Indoor pools + outdoor rotenburo in mountain garden. Rinse at shower station before entering. No swimwear. Hair up. ~42°C. Give 45–60 mins. Try both indoor and outdoor.

Tell reception at check-in you want late dinner. Orchid Room (historic Western dining, chandeliers, mountain views) or room service. Western course recommended — served since 1878. Local Hakone trout. Wear yukata — encouraged. ¥6,000–10,000/person.
Baths quieter now. Outdoor rotenburo at night — cool mountain air above, hot mineral water below, complete silence. One of the best things you'll do this trip.
Tomorrow (May 16) is the full Fujiya rest and onsen recovery day. Dawn rotenburo at 06:30. No buses, no climbing.

tips: Check fujisan.jp the night before for Mt Fuji visibility forecast
Chureito: stand LEFT of pagoda for the classic five-story + Fuji shot
Ropeway closes 17:00 — if you miss it, fold Owakudani into May 16 morning instead
Hakone Shrine at dusk is the best time — light extraordinary, crowds thin
Taxi from Hakone Shrine to Fujiya Hotel ¥2,000 — worth it after today's mileage

optional.hakone_open_air_museum: If arriving Kawaguchiko earlier than expected or Chureito goes quickly, add the Open Air Museum on the way to Fujiya. Chokoku-no-Mori Station on Tozan Railway — one stop before Miyanoshita. Open 09:00–17:00, last entry 16:30. Forest foot bath inside is the highlight. Hakone Free Pass gives discount (~¥1,600 after discount). Only if timing genuinely allows.

itinerary.label: Fujiya Checkout → Kyoto Arrival + Higashiyama Evening
itinerary.early_start_reason: Dawn onsen before checkout and travel to Kyoto

flags.fuji_view_alert: Possible last Mt Fuji view — RIGHT side Shinkansen ~13:45
flags.maiko_alert: 18:00-19:30 Hanamikoji Street — prime maiko hour on arrival evening

schedule.activity: Wake up — last morning at Fujiya Hotel
schedule.notes: Set alarm tonight before sleeping.

schedule.activity: ♨️ ONSEN 2 — Dawn rotenburo at Fujiya Hotel
schedule.notes: Almost empty at this hour. Mountain mineral water at sunrise. Outdoor rotenburo with misty forest views. This is also your farewell Hakone soak — savour it. No swimwear. Hair up.

schedule.activity: 🍳 Breakfast at Fujiya Hotel — Orchid Room
schedule.notes: Last meal here. Western or Japanese course. Served in the same room since 1878. Take your time.

schedule.activity: Pack bags + explore Fujiya Hotel grounds
schedule.notes: Wander in yukata one last time. Visit the memorabilia room (Chaplin, Lennon, Keller signatures). Gift shop for Fujiya Hotel souvenir tins.

schedule.activity: 🚃 Hakone Tozan Railway → Gora (Hakone Free Pass)
schedule.notes: One stop up from Miyanoshita (5 min). Last use of Hakone Free Pass.

schedule.activity: 🌸 Hakone Gora Park — final Hakone stop
schedule.notes: French-style garden 3 min from Gora Station. Roses and wisteria in May. 45 min gentle walk. Entry ~¥700.

schedule.activity: Return to Fujiya Hotel — collect luggage
schedule.notes: Tozan Railway back to Miyanoshita (5 min, Hakone Free Pass).
Standard checkout is 11:00 — ask at reception if a 12:00 late checkout is possible (worth trying). Store luggage at front desk if needed while you do the Gora detour.
~30 min total. Hakone Free Pass covers this leg.
Good ekiben (station bento) options in Odawara Station food hall. Quick lunch before Shinkansen.

schedule.activity: Reserve Hikari Shinkansen seats at Odawara JR office
schedule.notes: Show JR Pass. Ordinary reserved car. RIGHT side seats for Fuji view opportunity.
JR Pass. ~1h 40min. Ordinary reserved. RIGHT side — Mt Fuji visible ~13:45 between Odawara and Shin-Fuji. May be your last Fuji view of the trip. Camera ready!

schedule.activity: 🗻 Mt Fuji — RIGHT SIDE — possible last Fuji view!
schedule.notes: Between Odawara and Shin-Fuji stations. Look right. Clear days only — check fujisan.jp.
Welcome to Kyoto — one day earlier than planned!

schedule.activity: Taxi to Dormy Inn Kyoto Gion — check in
schedule.notes: 10-15 min taxi. Right at check-in time (15:00) so no waiting. Drop bags, freshen up.
10 min. Late afternoon light is beautiful on the temple approach.
Climb up through Ninenzaka. Wooden stage with sweeping city views. Late afternoon light hits the stage beautifully. Allow 45-50 min.
Stroll downhill through machiya shops. Matcha soft serve, ceramics, lacquerware. Golden hour light on the lanes. Allow 25-30 min.

schedule.activity: Yasaka Shrine — stone lanterns start glowing
schedule.notes: 10 min walk from base of Ninenzaka. Beautiful at dusk.

schedule.activity: Walk to Hanamikoji Street — 5 min from Yasaka
Gion — Hanamikoji Street — prime maiko hour!
schedule.notes: 18:00-19:30 is peak maiko time. Walk slowly, no flash photography, bow respectfully if you see one. You're already in Gion so hotel is 5 min walk away.

schedule.activity: Dinner in Gion area — first Kyoto meal!
schedule.notes: Pontocho is 10 min walk if you want the riverside kawadoko terrace (May = open season). Or stay in Gion — plenty of options within a few minutes.
Or walk — hotel is very close to Hanamikoji.

schedule.activity: ♨️ Dormy Inn Kyoto onsen — first Kyoto soak
schedule.notes: Open late. Good way to end arrival day.

tips: Ask Fujiya Hotel about late checkout to 12:00 at reception the night before — not guaranteed but worth asking
Hakone Free Pass covers the Gora detour and the Odawara journey — good final use of the pass
Right side seats on the Shinkansen from Odawara — Mt Fuji is visible ~13:45 on clear days
Kiyomizu-dera at 16:15 is great timing — late afternoon light on the wooden stage is stunning
Maiko spotting on Hanamikoji: 18:00-19:30, walk slowly, no flash, give them space

itinerary.label: Amanohashidate + Ine Fishing Village — Full Day Tour
itinerary.tour_note: Fully organised tour — no independent transport or booking required beyond the tour itself.

schedule.activity: 🌊 Amanohashidate + Ine Fishing Village — full day organised tour
schedule.notes: One of Japan's Three Views. Pine-covered sandbar + traditional funaya boathouses built directly over the water with boats parked under the living room. Tour operator handles all transport, entry, and logistics. Expect to be out roughly 08:00–19:00 depending on operator.

schedule.activity: Return to Dormy Inn Kyoto Gion
schedule.notes: Dinner in Gion or Pontocho on return.

tips: Amanohashidate is ~2 hrs from Kyoto — tour handles all pickup and transport
Ine funaya boathouses are uniquely Japanese — boats park directly under the living room floor
Book ASAP — May is peak season and day tours sell out weeks ahead

itinerary.label: Arashiyama + Tea Ceremony + Samurai Museum

schedule.notes: Drop large bags at hotel front desk before 9 AM for TA-Q-BIN pickup. Address: Residence Hotel Takayama Station. Bags arrive May 20 — travel with day packs only for May 19 + 20 morning.
10 min.
JR Sagano Line > Saga-Arashiyama (25 min). Walk deeper than 100m!
3 min walk from grove.
Shigetsu vegetarian or Yoshimura soba.

schedule.activity: JR Sagano > Kyoto Station > Karasuma Subway

schedule.notes: 8 min walk from Karasuma-Oike. Book at camellia-teaceremony.com!

schedule.activity: Nijo Castle - nightingale floors! UNESCO Heritage
schedule.notes: 10 min walk. Gold-leaf interiors + chirping floorboards.

schedule.activity: ⚔️ Samurai and Ninja Museum - armor + sword + shuriken
schedule.notes: Try on samurai armor, handle katana, throw shuriken!
Only available May-September. Perfect timing!

schedule.activity: Kamo River evening walk - sit on the stone steps
schedule.notes: Classic Kyoto evening moment.

tips: Nijo nightingale floors: walk as slowly and quietly as possible
Pontocho kawadoko terrace is only May-September - don't miss it

itinerary.label: Fushimi Inari + Kinkaku-ji + Osaka Day Trip
itinerary.last_night_note: Checking into Osaka tonight — pack bags before dinner.

schedule.activity: ⛩️ Fushimi Inari - climb to Yotsutsuji + beyond!
schedule.notes: Go PAST Yotsutsuji — most tourists turn back here. Upper trails are near-empty. ~1h 45min total.
~25 min, ~¥1,800. NOT JR Pass area — taxi is the practical link here.
Gold leaf shimmers best in morning light. ~45 min. Crowds build fast — good timing.

schedule.activity: Sekkatei teahouse matcha + exit matcha soft serve
schedule.notes: Right at the exit. Don't skip this.

schedule.activity: Taxi to Kyoto Station + quick lunch
schedule.notes: ~20 min. Grab ekiben (station bento) from the basement food hall — eat on the train.
JR Pass. 15 min. Hikari or Nozomi. Day trip — returning tonight.

schedule.activity: Arrive Shin-Osaka → taxi to Osaka Castle
Osaka Castle outer grounds — free walk
schedule.notes: Moats, stone walls, cherry blossom grounds. No entry fee for grounds.

schedule.activity: 🏯 Osaka Castle main tower — 8 floors + observation deck
schedule.notes: ¥600/person. Osaka panorama from the top. Allow 1 hour.

schedule.activity: JR Osaka Loop Line → Shin-Imamiya → JR Namba
schedule.notes: JR Pass. Loop Line to Shin-Imamiya, transfer Yamatoji Line one stop to JR Namba. ~25 min total.
Eat as you walk — tuna, wagyu skewers, giant oysters. ~1 hour.
30-40 min browse. Osaka's youth culture hub.
Quick walk-through. ~30 min.
Mandatory couple photo at the iconic neon sign. Canal reflections at dusk.
Kani Doraku (giant mechanical crab entrance) or Ichifuji takoyaki. First full Osaka meal!
Taxi ~20 min from Dotonbori, or subway + JR. Allow buffer.
JR Pass. 15 min. Last Hikari around 22:30 if you want more time.

schedule.activity: Arrive Kyoto Station → taxi to Dormy Inn Kyoto Gion
schedule.notes: ~15 min taxi. Staying one more night in Kyoto.

tips: Fushimi Inari: go early and past Yotsutsuji — crowds thin fast above the midpoint
Nishiki: 30 min graze only — you have Kinkaku-ji and a train to catch
Maruyama Park: 20-30 min farewell stroll — no need to linger
Pontocho kawadoko: book ahead if possible, May terrace season is busy
Pack bags before dinner — taxi straight to station after eating

itinerary.label: Checkout Kyoto → Wide View Hida → Takayama

schedule.notes: Lawson or FamilyMart near hotel. Coffee + onigiri. Quick — big travel day.

schedule.activity: Check out Dormy Inn Kyoto Gion
schedule.notes: Bags already forwarded via TA-Q-BIN on May 18. Day pack only.
10 min.
JR Pass. ~38 min. Heading east toward Tokyo direction. Reserved seat.
Head to Wide View Hida platform (JR Central). Allow 15-20 min for platform change.

schedule.activity: 🚂 Wide View Hida Limited Express — Nagoya → Takayama
schedule.notes: JR Pass. ~2h 25min. Panoramic windows. Kiso Valley gorge scenery. Seat tip: LEFT side for mountain river views.
Welcome to the Alps! Bags already waiting at hotel via TA-Q-BIN.

schedule.activity: Check in Residence Hotel Takayama Station
schedule.notes: 30 seconds from the station exit. Collect TA-Q-BIN bags.

schedule.activity: Lunch — Hida beef ramen or local soba
schedule.notes: Several spots 2 min walk from hotel in Sanmachi direction.

schedule.activity: Sanmachi Suji historic district + sake tastings
schedule.notes: Edo-period merchant town. Three parallel lanes of dark timber buildings. Free sake tastings at Hirase, Funasaka, Ota breweries.

schedule.activity: Takayama Jinya — only surviving Edo government house in Japan
schedule.notes: ¥450/person. 1692 magistrate's office with original rice granaries. 45 min.
2.4km walking trail linking 13 temples and shrines through forested hillside. About 1 hour.

schedule.activity: 🍽️ Dinner — Hida beef sukiyaki or hoba miso
schedule.notes: Hida beef is the local wagyu. Hoba miso (miso grilled on magnolia leaf) is unmissable. Try Kakusho or Jinya-mae area.

schedule.activity: Sanmachi Suji at night — lanterns + silence
schedule.notes: Completely transforms after dark. Most tourists gone. Stone lanterns glowing, total quiet.

tips: Dotonbori: stand on Ebisu Bridge at dusk facing west for the classic neon shot
Dormy Inn free ramen: served 21:30-23:00 nightly - do not miss it

itinerary.label: Morning Market + Hida Folk Village
itinerary.luggage_note: Send large bags via TA-Q-BIN to Residence Hotel Takayama this morning. Bags arrive May 22.

schedule.activity: Miyagawa Morning Market - running since Edo period
schedule.notes: 5 min walk. Bring CASH - most stalls cash only!

schedule.activity: Morning Sanmachi Suji - mist + quiet lanes
Kusakabe Folk Museum - 1879 preserved merchant house
Cedar Avenue (Sugi-namiki) - 360 year old cryptomeria trees
schedule.notes: 15 min walk. Almost no tourists!

schedule.activity: Lunch at Amazake-chaya - 400yr old thatched teahouse
schedule.notes: Open since 1638!
Walk inside every open farmhouse! Allow 1.5-2 hrs.

schedule.activity: Final Sanmachi - sake shopping + crafts + Hida beef manju
Dinner - Hida beef sukiyaki + hoba miso + local sake
Final Takayama night walk - lanterns + total silence
schedule.notes: Unforgettable.

schedule.activity: Back to hotel - pack for Tokyo tomorrow
schedule.notes: Set alarm for 6:50 AM.

tips: Wide View Hida: LEFT side A/B seats for gorge views - reserve at any JR office
Sanmachi Suji at night is one of Japan's most beautiful quiet experiences - don't skip it

itinerary.label: Checkout Takayama → Tokyo + Super Autobacs
itinerary.last_night_note: Last night in Takayama - pack for Tokyo tomorrow!
itinerary.luggage_note: Large bags arrive from Osaka today! Collect at front desk.

schedule.notes: Convenience store near station or hotel lobby. Quick — trains to catch.

schedule.activity: Check out Residence Hotel Takayama Station
schedule.notes: Day pack or full bags — TA-Q-BIN option: send bags to Yuen Shinjuku from hotel desk before 09:00.
JR Pass. ~2h 30min. Last mountain train — enjoy the Hida river gorge.
Quick transfer to Shinkansen platforms (JR Central concourse).
Hitsumabushi eel or miso katsu bento from the basement food hall. ~20 min.
JR Pass. ~1h 45min. Ordinary reserved.
JR Chuo Line rapid → Shinjuku (~20 min). Or taxi direct to Yuen Shinjuku.
Kabukicho / Shinjuku area. Drop bags, freshen up.
Nearest large stores: Super Autobacs SEVEN Kasai (~35 min by train/taxi) or check autobacs.com for closest open location. Multi-floor car parts heaven.
Omoide Yokocho (Memory Lane) for yakitori, or Tsunahachi tempura nearby.
Race day. Set alarm for 06:00.

tips: Morning Market: 7 AM sharp for freshest produce. Bring cash - cash only stalls.
Buy sake DIRECT from breweries - namazake (unpasteurized) unavailable outside Hida

itinerary.label: SUZUKA CIRCUIT — Super Formula Day Trip from Tokyo

schedule.notes: Early start. Grab breakfast from Lawson/FamilyMart on the way to the station.
Yuen Shinjuku is ~5 min walk to Shinjuku Station.
JR Pass — take Hikari NOT Nozomi (JR Pass not valid on Nozomi). ~2h 10min. Depart from Tokyo or Shinagawa Station.
Exit toward Kintetsu Nagoya Station (underground connection, ~5 min walk).

schedule.activity: Buy Kintetsu tickets — Nagoya → Shiroko + return
schedule.notes: NOT JR Pass. Buy BOTH outbound and return at same window. ~¥1,720 each way. Kintetsu Limited Express ticket + seat reservation.
NOT JR Pass — separate ticket. ~40 min. Sit back and get excited.

schedule.activity: Arrive Shiroko Station — follow signs to shuttle
schedule.notes: English signs to Mie Kotsu shuttle bus. ~¥400. Runs frequently on race day.
Race Day Special Passport + Pit Walk Pass at gate. Pick up circuit map.

schedule.activity: Explore circuit — Motopia Park + Racing Theater + Ferris Wheel
schedule.notes: Get your bearings. Walk the outer areas. The Ferris Wheel gives a circuit overview.

schedule.activity: 🏎️ PIT WALK — walk the actual Suzuka pitlane!
schedule.notes: Pit Walk Pass required. Stand next to the cars, see the mechanics, pit lane close-up.
Circuit food stalls — Japanese race day food. Eat early before the crowds.
Find grandstand seats early. Sound is incredible — no earplugs needed but bring them just in case.

schedule.activity: Head to shuttle bus — leave before the rush
schedule.notes: Leave 15 min before the final session ends to beat the crowd to the shuttle.
~20 min.
NOT JR Pass — use return ticket bought this morning.
Optional: quick Nagoya-style dinner (miso katsu, hitsumabushi) before the train.
JR Pass. ~1h 45min. Back to Shinjuku by ~21:00.
JR Chuo Line ~20 min to Shinjuku, or taxi direct.

schedule.activity: 🍽️ Late dinner — Omoide Yokocho or Kabukicho ramen
schedule.notes: Celebrate the race day!
Best views of the trip — Tokyo skyline at midnight. Open late.

tips: Kintetsu is NOT JR Pass - buy at Kintetsu office EAST side Nagoya Station
Buy BOTH outbound and return Kintetsu tickets at the same time
Pit Walk: no umbrellas allowed in the pitlane - pack a poncho

itinerary.label: A-PIT JDM + Harajuku + Shopping

schedule.activity: Check out - store bags at hotel front desk
Rinkai Line from Shinjuku > Shinonome (via Osaki)
schedule.notes: 35 min.

schedule.activity: 🚗 A-PIT Super Autobacs - JDM paradise! 4 floors
schedule.notes: Walk the PARKING LOT first - show cars change regularly. 3 min walk from Shinonome station.
35 min.

schedule.activity: Lunch in Harajuku or Omotesando area
Cat Street - Off-White, Supreme Japan, BAPE, Neighborhood
schedule.notes: Japan-only releases not in US stores!

schedule.activity: Ura-Harajuku boutiques - Sacai, Kapital, Nanamica, Beams Japan

schedule.notes: 7 min.

schedule.activity: Isetan Shinjuku - B1 food hall + craft floor + menswear

schedule.notes: Check back in for second night.

schedule.activity: Dinner - New York Bar Park Hyatt or Kozue
schedule.notes: Optional splurge. Lost in Translation vibes.
2 min from hotel.

schedule.activity: Yuen Shinjuku rooftop onsen - last onsen of the trip
schedule.notes: Savour it.

tips: A-PIT parking lot first before going inside - the show cars are the highlight
Cat Street: Supreme Japan has Japan-only releases different from US stores
Ask every store for tax-free shopping - show passport for 10% refund on Y5,000+ purchases

itinerary.label: Takashimaya + Final Shopping + Depart

schedule.activity: Wake up - final morning in Japan
schedule.notes: Last breakfast in Japan!

schedule.activity: Check out Yuen Shinjuku - store bags at front desk

schedule.notes: 8 min walk from hotel.

schedule.activity: Takashimaya B1-B2 food hall - Kit Kats, wagashi, sake, matcha, yuzu
schedule.notes: Best souvenir shopping in Tokyo.

schedule.activity: Takashimaya 6F craft floor - ceramics, lacquerware, washi, knives
Takashimaya 8F tax-free counter - 10% refund, bring passport!
schedule.notes: Consolidate ALL purchases here.

schedule.activity: Shinjuku final shopping - Beams Japan + Tokyu Hands
Final Tokyo lunch - Afuri Ramen or Tsunahachi tempura

schedule.notes: 8 min walk.

schedule.activity: N'EX Narita Express - Shinjuku > Narita Airport
schedule.notes: 90 min. JR Pass. DEPART ON TIME - tight connection at Narita.
Go straight to Air Canada check-in.
Counter closes before 16:30. Move quickly.
Allow 20-25 min.

schedule.activity: At gate - last Japan duty free!
schedule.notes: Yamazaki, Hibiki, Nikka whisky. Best prices in Japan.
Boeing 787-9. 8h 45m. Wi-Fi. Meal + Breakfast included.
You crossed the Date Line going east - gain a day back.
Boeing 737 MAX 8. 4h 27m.
Welcome home! Uber back to Cedar Park ~1 hour.

tips: N'EX at 14:20 is non-negotiable - missing it means missing the flight
Return Suica cards at Narita JR office for remaining balance + Y500 deposit
Whisky: Narita duty free usually has Yamazaki and Hibiki even when shops are sold out

bookings.alert: TICKETS ON SALE: Saturday April 11 at 9:00 PM Chicago time. Buy Race Day Special Passport for May 23 + Pit Walk Pass.

bookings.where: Purchase before leaving Austin - cannot buy in Japan

bookings.item: Fujikyuko Highway Bus: Shinjuku → Kawaguchiko (07:45, May 15)
Amanohashidate + Ine Fishing Village full day tour (May 17)
bookings.when: Book ASAP — May is peak season
bookings.notes: Search "Amanohashidate Ine day tour from Kyoto". May is peak — book ASAP.

bookings.item: Fujikyuko Highway Bus: Kawaguchiko → Gotemba → Hakone-Yumoto (14:15, May 15)

experiences.item: Kimono dress-up at Wargo Asakusa
Sumo Natsu Basho - Makuuchi top division bouts
Super Formula live racing + pit walk at Suzuka Circuit
JDM A-PIT Super Autobacs - 4 floors
teamLab Planets immersive digital art
Chureito Pagoda - iconic Mt Fuji view
Mt Fuji Panoramic Ropeway + ring the love bell
Odakyu Romance Car scenic train ride
Gran Class Shinkansen - Odawara to Kyoto
Fujiya Hotel mineral onsen x3 (evenings + dawn)
Tenzan Tohji-kyo forest public onsen
Authentic matcha tea ceremony 90 min at Camellia
Samurai Museum - armor fitting + sword + shuriken
Fushimi Inari thousand torii gates
Kinkaku-ji the Golden Pavilion
Gion Hanamikoji Street and spotting maiko
Osaka Castle 8 floors + observation deck
Dotonbori neon canal + Glico Man couple photo
Sanmachi Suji Takayama historic lanes + sake tastings
Hida Folk Village gassho-zukuri thatched farmhouses
Harajuku Cat Street - Supreme Japan, BAPE, Off-White
Takashimaya Times Square food hall + craft floor
Narita duty free Japanese whisky
Amanohashidate pine sandbar — one of Japan's Three Views
Ine funaya fishing village boathouses

documents.item: JR Pass vouchers x2 - in carry-on ONLY, never checked bag
Air Canada booking confirmation (AC 1763 + AC 3 / AC 4 + AC 1762)
Suzuka Circuit Race Day Special Passport + Pit Walk Pass

essentials.item: Portable WiFi / pocket WiFi (pre-book for Japan)
Universal power adapter (Japan uses Type A - same plug as US)
Cash in USD to exchange on arrival
Comfortable walking shoes (10,000+ steps per day)
Slip-on shoes for May 13 (kimono + teamLab water rooms)
Small daypack for TA-Q-BIN forwarding days (May 15 + May 21)
Small towel for onsen (Fujiya + Dormy Inn provide towels)

clothing.item: Smart casual for Gran Class train and Fujiya Hotel dining
Easy-remove shoes for temples, tea ceremony, tatami rooms

tech.item: Camera + extra batteries and memory cards
Download Japan maps offline in Google Maps (do on home WiFi)
Download Japanese language pack in Google Translate (do on home WiFi)

transport_tips: JR Pass covers all Shinkansen except Nozomi and Mizuho - always book Hikari or Kodama
Kintetsu to Suzuka is NOT JR Pass - buy separately at Kintetsu office east side Nagoya Station
Suica card works on all non-JR metro lines, buses, and even convenience stores and vending machines
IC card readers everywhere - tap in and tap out
Last train typically around midnight in major cities - do not miss it
etiquette_tips: No eating or drinking while walking (except at food stalls and festivals)
Trash cans are rare - carry a small bag for waste
Quiet on trains - no phone calls, keep music in headphones
Onsen rules: shower before entering, no swimwear, tattoos may be restricted at some
Cash is still widely used - always carry some yen
Remove shoes when entering temples, traditional restaurants, and some museums

apps_to_download.name: Google Maps
apps_to_download.purpose: Navigation + download Japan offline maps before leaving

apps_to_download.name: Google Translate
apps_to_download.purpose: Camera translate menus and signs (download Japanese offline)
